question:
Will FlyQuest win LoL Worlds 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the esports team winning 1st place in the League of Legends 2025 Season World Championship (Worlds 2025).

If the winner of the World Cup 2025 is not determined by December 31, 2025 ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Liquipedia: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.
event_title:

If the winner of the World Cup 2025 is not determined by December 31, 2025 ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Liquipedia: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.

evidences.title: 2025 World Championship
evidences.content: View match details ## Format [edit] Qualification MSI champion qualifies into Worlds if they advance to their regional playoffs The top three teams besides MSI champion from each region (15 total) participate in Swiss Stage The 4th seed of MSI winning region (LCK) & runner-up region (LPL) start from Play-In Play-In - _October 14 th_ The 4th seed of the regions that obtained an additional slot from MSI compete in a single elimination match. Winner qualifies into Swiss Stage. The match is Bo5 using Fearless Draft. [...] Swiss Stage - _October 15 th - 19 th, 22 nd - 25 th_ Sixteen teams play in a 5 round Swiss format. Teams that reach 3 wins advance to Knockout Stage. Teams that reach 3 losses are eliminated. Elimination and advancement matches are Bo3 using Fearless Draft All other matches are Bo1. Side Selection for Game 1: 1. Higher regional seed picks sides 2. If both teams have the same regional seed, the team drawn first picks sides [...] Contents 1 Prize Pool 2 Participating Teams 3 Results 3.1 Play-In 3.2 Swiss Stage 3.3 Knockout Stage 4 Format 4.1 Worlds 2025 Draw Show 5 Broadcasts 5.1 Streams 5.2 Broadcast Talent 6 Additional Data 6.1 Champion Statistics 6.2 Country Repre...

evidences.title: Worlds 2025 Format Explained: Everything You Need To Know
evidences.content: Each of these two regions will field a total of three teams in this tournament, just like the LEC. The LPL and the LCK, after being the top two regions at MSI, were awarded a fourth additional slot, although the two #4 seeds will have to battle it out in the play-in stage to qualify for the Swiss stage, the first official stage of Worlds 2025. According to the official schedule, the Swiss stage will kick off on October 15. ## Worlds 2025 Format is split into 3 stages ### Stage 1: Play-In Stage 2 Teams A single best-of-five series between LPL and LCK #4 seeds featuring Fearless Draft. Winners will advance to the Swiss stage, losers will be eliminated and go home early. ### Stage 2: Swiss Stage [...] ### Stage 2: Swiss Stage Introduced in 2023 to replace the group stage, the Swiss stage format has the main goal of creating higher-stakes matches and increase the cross-regional competition right from the start. 16 Teams The teams play up to five rounds. Each round, teams will play against an opponent with the same win-loss record. Matches are best-of-one until elimination or qualification matches (2 losses or 2 wins), which will then shift to best-of-threes. The 8 teams to get 3 win...

evidences.title: All LoL Worlds Champions
evidences.content: China’s LPL has emerged as a strong challenger. Chinese teams have won 3 World Championships: Invictus Gaming (2018), FunPlus Phoenix (2019), and Edward Gaming (2021). Invictus Gaming’s victory in 2018 was historic as it was the first-ever Worlds title for China, breaking the Korean streak that had reigned since the first Worlds Championship. LoL Worlds Winners LoL Worlds Winners North America (NA) is still chasing its first World Championship. No NA team has ever won Worlds, and in fact, no North American team has even reached the finals. The best NA performances have been reaching semifinals (Cloud9 in 2018’s top four). [...] Q: Who won LoL Worlds each year? A: The world champions for every year are listed above in our complete winners table. In brief: Fnatic won the inaugural World Championship in 2011; Taipei Assassins won in 2012; SK Telecom T1 (T1) dominated 2013, 2015, 2016; Samsung White took 2014; Samsung Galaxy won 2017; Invictus Gaming and FunPlus Phoenix brought home 2018 and 2019 respectively for China; DAMWON Gaming won 2020; Edward Gaming won 2021; DRX won 2022; and T1 were champions in 2023 and 2024. Q: Which team has the most LoL Worlds titles? A: South Korea’s...

evidences.title: Everything YOU Need to Know About Worlds 2025 - YouTube
evidences.content: played in game one are banned for game two. The 20 champions in game 1 and two are banned for game three, and so on. For the Swiss stage, it works the same as it did in 2023 and 2024. The 16 teams are all given a seed based on how they performed in their region, with winners given the one seed, runner-ups the two seed, and third place the three seed. Depending on who wins the playin match, either the LCK or LPL3 seed will be promoted to two seed for this withdraw. This way, there are five one seeds, six two seeds, and five three seeds. In round one, one seeds will be drawn against three seeds, and two seeds will play against other two seeds in a best of one. The way Swiss works is this. If you win a match, you move up to 1 and 0. And if you lose, you go to 0 and one. Every team who has [...] never looked that high in the LCK CL and the signing looked weird. However, it turned out Pobe's style ended up complimenting the team very well, and they've finally taken steps towards playing like a stable team. evidences.title: Worlds 2025 Primer - LoL Esports
evidences.content: PSG Talon (PSG) WORLDS FORMAT Video 1 SWISS STAGE DRAWS Right after the Play-In Stage wraps, the action jumps straight into the Swiss Draws, with matchups revealed live and the first sparks of Worlds drama igniting. Round 1 (October 14) The first round is all based on seeding. This year, we’ve changed up our draw tiers and added in a new twist - the Play-In result doesn’t just decide who qualifies to Swiss, it also bumps that region’s #3 seed into Tier 2. Teams are split into three tiers: Tier 1 (5 Teams): #1 seed from each region Tier 2 (6 Teams): #2 seed from each region + the #3 seed from the Play-In winner’s region Tier 3 (5 Teams): Remaining #3 seeds + the Play-In winner Matchups will be: Tier 1 vs Tier 3 Tier 2 vs Tier 2 [...] Play-In Stage (Bo5) - Beijing(All matches begin at 4 p.m. CST / 10 a.m. CET / 1 a.m. PT unless otherwise noted) Tuesday, October 14 Swiss Stage (Bo1, Bo3)- Beijing (All matches begin at 4 p.m. CST / 10 a.m. CET / 1 a.m. PT unless otherwise noted) Wednesday, October 15 - 1 p.m. CST / 7 a.m. CET / 10 p.m. PT Oct. 14 Thursday, October 16 - 1 p.m. CST / 7 a.m. CET / 10 p.m. PT Oct. 15 Friday, October 17 Saturday, October 18 Sunday, October 19 Wednesday,...

evidences.title: LoL Esports |
evidences.content: Lyon Gaming: Six Karma y Movistar R7, dos competidores de la LLA, se unen para traer de regreso una marca icónica en la historia de Latinoamérica como parte de la conferencia norte en 2025, como el socio oficial de Latinoamérica. Cloud9: Cloud9 fue el primer equipo norteamericano desde la temporada 1 en alcanzar las semifinales en Worlds, logrando este hito histórico en 2018 y solidificando su posición en la historia de los esports de la LCS. Dignitas: Dignitas era uno de los equipos originales de la LCS en 2013, conocido por formar a talentos destacados como Scarra e Imaqtpie y por dar forma al panorama competitivo inicial de League of Legends en Norteamérica. [...] FlyQuest: fundado en 2017, FlyQuest se dio a conocer por sus iniciativas "Go Green" y su ascenso competitivo, llegando a dos finales consecutivas en 2020 antes de conseguir su primer título de campeón de la LCS en 2024. FLY también logró clasificarse para los cuartos de final de Worlds en 2024. Shopify Rebellion: Shopify Rebellion entró en la LCS en 2024, debutando con la adquisición de la antigua plantilla de TSM y estableciéndose rápidamente como una fuerza competitiva. Están listos para continuar en 2025, con el...

evidences.title: All LoL Worlds winners throughout the years
evidences.content: ## Worlds 2022 The Chase Center in San Francisco was a special place for the Koreans DRX in 2022. More than 70% of the fans, according to Riot, thought that T1 was going to win the Worlds 2022. However, when DRX was losing 2-0 and everything seemed lost, they did a reverse sweep and won the Worlds by 3-2. The top laner Hwang “Kingen” Seong-hoon was elected the MVP of the event. DRX won the Worlds 2022 (Image via Riot Games) ## Worlds 2023 In the baseball stadium, Gocheok Sky Dome in Seoul, T1 took their revenge and finally won the Worlds after many losses throughout the year. Against Weibo Gaming, T1 won by 3-0. The top laner Choi “Zeus” Woo-je was elected the MVP of the Worlds. T1 won the Worlds 2023 (Image via Riot Games) ## Worlds 2024 [...] The Korean team DAMWON Gaming (previously DWG KIA, now named Dplus Kia) defeated the Chinese team Suning by 3-1. The MVP was the jungler Kim “Canyon” Geon-bu, nowadays playing for Gen.G. DAMWON Gaming won the Worlds 2020 (Image via Riot Games) ## Worlds 2021 The winners of 2020 couldn’t keep the victory for another year in the Worlds. The Chinese team EDward Gaming won against DWG KIA by 3-2. The finals were in Laugardalshöll Sports Cente...

LOLWORLDS 28.03.25 - 14:4828.03.2025 - 14:48·10m10 minute(s) read· ByClément Chocat # LoL: All Worlds Winners This article chronicles every winner of the League of Legends World Championship from its inception in 2011 through 2024, highlighting each team's journey to victory, major upsets, historic milestones, and the evolving dominance of regions like South Korea’s LCK, China’s LPL, and early breakthroughs by Europe and the PCS. 00 Share Summoner's Cup Summoner's Cup ## Sheep Esports presents all League of Legends World Champions, year by year, since Worlds began in 2011 Loading... Loading... [...] Loading... Loading... The League of Legends World Championship, or Worlds, has seen 9 different organizations claim victory across its 14 editions. The LCK leads with an impressive 9 titles, while the LPL has won 3 times. Europe and the PCS have each secured 1 title. When it comes to individual achievements, Lee "Faker" Sang-hyeok stands as the most successful player, with a record 9 appearances at Worlds. Alongside his team, T1, he has claimed the trophy 5 times and reached 7 fi...

evidences.title: League of Legends: Every Worlds champion crowned
evidences.content: ### 2024 Champions – T1 The T1 with trophy The T1 line up during the Lol Worlds 2024 victory was Lee “Gumayusi” Min-hyeong, Choi “Zeus” Woo-je, Ryu “Keria” Min-seok, Moon “Oner” Hyeon-joon and Lee “Faker” Sang-hyeok / Image credit: Riot Games and League of Legends Worlds At the time of writing, the most recent League of Legends World Championship win was in 2024, and it was another victory for T1, who earned $450,000 in winnings from the $2,225,000 prize pool. The team showed off its usual strategic prowess and excellent macro plays, achieving a 3–1 record in the Swiss Stage and a 3-0 sweep in the quarter-finals. They faced off against Gen.G in the semi-finals and Bilibili Gaming in the finals (both of which are teams that could stand a chance of winning in 2025). [...] The Invictus Gaming with trophy The Invictus Gaming line up during the Lol Worlds 2018 victory was Yu “JackeyLove” Wen-Bo, Gao “Ning” Zhen-Ning, Wang “Baolan” Liu-Yi, Song “Rookie” Eui-jin, and Kang “TheShy” Seung-Iok / Image credit: Riot Games and League of Legends Worlds Invictus Gaming managed to beat the very first winners of the LoL World Championships (Fnatic) in 2018 to become the first Chinese team to cla...

The first Nexus tower's fallen and T1 are just looking for the prize. They're hungry for it. T1 are the past, the present, and the future. a new the same empire. T1 are your 2024 world champions. And that concludes all of the current winners from every League of Legends World Championship. From the first Summoner Cup winner, Fanatic all the way to T1's most recent win claiming their fifth Summoner Cup. But the question is now, who will be next? Will the LPL continue their rivalry with the LCK, or will another region rise up and steal the spotlight? And the big question every fan asks year after year, will North America ever win the Summoner Cup? Only time will tell, but [...] The LoL Worlds tournament is the most prestigious event in esports, where the best teams across the globe compete for the Summoner’s Cup. Over the years, we’ve witnessed unforgettable storylines, MVP performances, and regional rivalries between Korea, China, Europe, and North America. evidences.title: League of Legends Championship of the Americas (LTA): Teams, format and details | Nerd Street
evidences.content: Photo credit: Riot Games Fluxo, FURIA, LOUD, paiN Gaming, Red Canids and Vivo Keyd Stars were all playoff teams in Split 2 of the 2024 CBLoL season with paiN earning a trip to Worlds. PaiN have been one of the most successful teams in CBLoL history. LOUD have been the standard-bearers for Brazil in recent years having gone to Worlds in 2022 and 2023 and MSI in 2023 and 2024. FURIA is one of the biggest Brazilian esports brands, and Red Canids have had a lot of success in CBLoL in the past. [...] Photo credit: Marv Watson / Riot Games It isn’t surprising to see Team Liquid and FlyQuest in the LTA North. These are two of the LCS teams that qualified for the League of Legends World Championship this year. Cloud9 is a longtime very successful member of the LCS. Dignitas is another longtime LCS member, though they have never had much success in the league. Shopify is the newest member of the LCS having acquired TSM’s spot this year.
